etymology.description: 䜌 (phonetic) + 心("heart") = romantic feelings -> love/romance
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 戀 combines the phonetic element 䜌 with the semantic 心 (xīn, "heart") to convey the concept of feelings associated with the heart, thus representing love or romance.
etymology.simplification: 戀(戀) was simplified to 恋(戀) where the phonetic component 䜌 was omitted, maintaining the semantic core with 心 (heart).

examples.chinese: 他们的恋情让人羡慕(他們的戀情讓人羨慕)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen de liànqíng ràng rén xiànmù.

examples.chinese: 我对她的恋慕使我每一天都期待未来(我對她的戀慕使我每一天都期待未來)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ duì tā de liànmù shǐ wǒ měi yītiān dōu qídài wèilái.
examples.english: My love for her makes me look forward to every day.

examples.chinese: 爱情是甜蜜的(愛情是甜蜜的)，恋人总是牵手散步(戀人總是牽手散步)。
examples.pinyin: Àiqíng shì tiánmì de, liànrén zǒngshì qiānshǒu sànbù.
examples.english: Love is sweet, lovers always walk hand in hand.
